_comment: Version-bump recipe for /bump and /release (see ~/.claude/skills/bump). Source of truth is src/finbreak/__init__.py __version__. 'files' are mechanical exact-string edits; the CHANGELOG entry cuts the accumulated [Unreleased] block into a dated section (bodies are authored during dev via changelog_log, so no todo is needed). post_check asserts every version-bearing file agrees. README.md's 'Current version' line is bumped mechanically (files) and drift-gated (post_check); its feature/'what's new' prose is refreshed via a todo at bump time so it stays layman-friendly (user directive 2026-07-12). NOTE: this recipe covers the version bump only -- the signed AppImage build + publish is a separate manual step (scripts/build-release-appimage.sh, verify the .sig against RELEASE_PUBLIC_KEY_B64, then gh release create v<NEW> with the AppImage + .sig attached). WINDOWS (FIBR-0131): the .exe self-updater needs a SIGNED .exe release asset -- build finbreak.exe via windows-build.yml / scripts/build-windows-exe.py, rename it to finbreak-<NEW>-x86_64.exe (the name MUST match WindowsInstaller.asset_suffix() '-x86_64.exe' or the updater won't find it -- no automated guard), sign it with `python scripts/sign-release.py finbreak-<NEW>-x86_64.exe` (Ed25519, same key as the AppImage), then attach BOTH the .exe and .exe.sig to the release. (v0.1.9 shipped the .exe but no .exe.sig, so the Windows updater is inert until a release carries the .sig.) Authenticode/SmartScreen publisher signing is separate (FIBR-0133).
post_check: V=$(sed -n 's/^__version__ = "\([0-9.]*\)"/\1/p' src/finbreak/__init__.py); test -n "$V" && grep -q "^version = \"$V\"$" pyproject.toml && grep -q "__version__ == \"$V\"" tests/test_smoke.py && grep -q "^## \[$V\] - " CHANGELOG.md && grep -qF "Current version: **$V**" README.md && grep -q "release version=\"$V\"" packaging/obs/io.github.milnet01.finbreak.metainfo.xml && grep -q "^finbreak ($V)" packaging/obs/debian/changelog && echo "version lockstep OK: $V" || { echo "VERSION DRIFT: expected $V across __init__.py, pyproject.toml, tests/test_smoke.py, README.md, a dated CHANGELOG section, the metainfo <release>, and the deb changelog"; exit 1; }
todos: Refresh README.md for v{NEW} so it stays LAYMAN-FRIENDLY (plain language, no jargon): update the feature list / 'what's new' wording to reflect the user-visible changes in the new [{NEW}] CHANGELOG section. The 'Current version' line is bumped mechanically by the files step (so just confirm it reads {NEW}); this todo is only the prose. Keep it short and outcome-focused -- what the app now does for the user, not how it works internally. ALSO sweep the standing status sections for staleness even when NO CHANGELOG line names them (they drift between releases): the **Code signing** note, the per-platform **Install** notes and their auto-update claims (Windows / macOS / Linux), and the distribution channels -- verify each against reality (roadmap ship-status, the actual release assets, whether SignPath/Authenticode is active). User directive 2026-07-23: ensure README.md is up to date on EVERY release, not just the feature list.

Sync the AppStream metainfo (FIBR-0155): prepend a `<release version="{NEW}" date="{TODAY}">` block to packaging/obs/io.github.milnet01.finbreak.metainfo.xml, generated from the new [{NEW}] CHANGELOG section via the changelog-writer agent (Mode A: CHANGELOG section -> AppStream <release> XML -- the /bump template's existing 'prepend <release>' todo). The newest <release> must equal __version__ (obs_packaging INV-6); post_check now gates this.

Sync the deb changelog (FIBR-0155): prepend a stanza to packaging/obs/debian/changelog NON-INTERACTIVELY (not `dch` -- no TTY): first line `finbreak ({NEW}) unstable; urgency=medium`, then the [{NEW}] CHANGELOG bullets as `  * ` lines, then a ` -- Anthony Schemel <aant.schemel@gmail.com>  <RFC-2822 date>` trailer (DEBFULLNAME/DEBEMAIL match the release key owner). changelog-writer has no deb mode, so write it mechanically. Its version must equal __version__ (obs_packaging INV-6); post_check now gates this.
